candidates.title: Tesla Commits $25 Billion to AI and Robotics Expansion
candidates.description: Elon Musk announced Tesla's plan to increase its 2026 capital expenditure to over $25 billion, primarily targeting the production of Optimus humanoid robots, Cybercab vehicles, and massive AI training clusters. This substantial investment signifies Tesla's deep commitment to AI-driven hardware and infrastructure.

candidates.title: Meta Pivots Workforce Towards AI Infrastructure with 8,000 Job Cuts
candidates.description: Meta announced a reduction of approximately 8,000 positions (10% of its workforce) to reallocate resources towards AI infrastructure and the development of next-generation large language models. This move highlights a major strategic shift in one of the world's largest tech companies, emphasizing AI as its core future direction.

candidates.title: UK Government Warns of Soaring CO2 Emissions from AI Data Centers
candidates.description: The UK government released data projecting that AI data centers could emit up to 123 million tonnes of CO2 over the next decade, a 100-fold increase over previous estimates. This report sparks significant debate over the environmental impact of the AI boom and is expected to influence future policy and sustainable AI development.

candidates.title: Google Announces Up to $40 Billion Investment in Anthropic
candidates.description: Google revealed plans to invest up to $40 billion in AI firm Anthropic, starting with an initial $10 billion commitment. This massive investment further deepens the strategic alliance between the two companies, significantly impacting the competitive landscape and infrastructure development in the AI industry.
